scenario: A Junction Retail operator running Camunda 8.8 has staged 5 modifications on one open process instance worth 320 euros of returns. Before applying anything the operator wants to bring up a single screen that lists every pending modification on this instance so they can review the change set together.
question: Which action in modification mode opens the summary modal listing every pending modification on the instance?

options.text: The operator clicks the Apply Modifications button in the footer of modification mode, and Operate opens a modal where all pending modifications staged on the instance can be reviewed together
The operator opens the Operate global notifications drawer at the top of the page, where a per-instance section accumulates the staged modifications as the operator stages each one
The operator selects each individual flow node with a pending change, and a side panel automatically refreshes to render only those modifications that touched the currently selected flow node
The operator presses an Undo button repeatedly until each change is briefly described in a toast, which lets the operator inspect the full pending list before re-staging the changes again

a.text: Correct. The docs say that to display pending modifications, the operator clicks Apply Modifications in the footer; a modal then opens where all of the modifications on the instance can be seen and managed before commit.

b.text: Incorrect. The pending modifications summary is opened from the modification footer, not from a global notifications drawer at the top of the Operate page.

c.text: Incorrect. The summary modal lists every pending modification together; there is no documented side panel that scopes the view down to a single selected flow node.

d.text: Incorrect. Undo from the modification footer reverses the latest modification rather than opening the pending summary, and it is not described as a way to enumerate the full pending list.

explanation: Clicking Apply Modifications in the modification-mode footer opens the summary modal. The modal lists every pending modification staged on the instance and lets the operator review and act on them before committing.
